ops.body:
Three items surfaced by the Wave 0 refuter round (2026-07-18) to fold into Wave 4 security hardening, alongside the XPC transport / XPCTrust / TF9 / TF10 work:

1. **Go reads the full frame before running trust.** wire/server.go (~272-324) reads a peer's frame (bounded by MaxLine) before the trust check runs — deliberate, so rejections can carry a reply, but it exposes frame parsing to untrusted same-UID peers. Swift's SocketServer checks PeerTrust before any read. Revisit the ordering when the XPC transport lands (per-message identity makes pre-read checks natural).

2. **Swift PeerTrust is weaker than Go's default policy.** PeerTrust takes a raw designated-requirement string; Go's darwin verifier additionally enforces Hardened Runtime and rejects injection entitlements (allow-jit included). Port requireHardenedRuntime + the injectionEntitlements rejection to Swift for parity (flagged by the L0d lane itself and the R7 refuter).

3. **Swift foreign-UID floor rejection is untested.** The EUID floor's negative path needs a cross-uid E2E like Go's DAEMONKIT_TRUST_E2E fixtures; the Swift check reads euid live from the fd (no injection seam, by design), so only a real second-uid connection can exercise it.

R7 otherwise verified the Swift posture CLOSED: floor always runs and is unconfigurable, every requirement error branch fails closed, requirement augments the floor, rejection precedes any frame read.

---

## Wave 4 DISPOSITION (2026-07-19)

**Item 1 — read-frame-before-trust ordering: SUBSUMED by the XPC transport (db24393).** The E1 spike proved the deeper problem: `LOCAL_PEERTOKEN` is query-time-LIVE, not connection-bound (XNU `uipc_ctloutput` does `proc_find(last_pid)+task_info(TASK_AUDIT_TOKEN)` on every call; demonstrated live by a same-pid+socket fork/exec substitution). So re-ordering trust-before-frame-read only marginally narrows exposure of the (bounded, same-UID) frame parser — it does NOT close the identity-substitution gap, because send-then-exec still substitutes even with trust-before-any-read. The real fix is the connection/message-bound XPC audit-token transport, GO'd on db24393. The current unsound boundary is now pinned by documented-limitation tests (trust/limitation_darwin_test.go: TF1 tested live, TF2/TF5 documented-skipped), which flip to rejection tests when the XPC transport lands. Standalone re-ordering is not worth a change on its own.

**Item 2 — Swift PeerTrust parity: RESOLVED.** Shipped to daemonkit main (CI green). Swift PeerTrust now enforces Hardened Runtime (CS_RUNTIME required; CS_GET_TASK_ALLOW / CS_DEBUGGED rejected) and rejects all six injection entitlements — allow-jit and the rest reject even under forced library validation; only disable-library-validation is LV-exempt — with allowUnhardened the sole bypass (never relaxes the DR), failing closed on missing/undecodable signing info. Sol security review found + fixed 2 real issues (a CFNumber(0)-value bypass of the boolean-false check; a fail-open on non-dictionary entitlements). 42 socket/E2E tests + 17+2 synthetic gate cases (HardenedRuntimeGateCases.swift) prove every branch on every push; the live signed-peer E2E is env-gated (DAEMONKIT_TRUST_E2E=1) like the Go side.

**Item 3 — Swift foreign-UID floor cross-uid E2E: PARTIAL, honest gap named.** The floor exists and is tested on the positive/same-UID path (selfConnectionPassesTheEUIDFloor + the server round-trip). The negative cross-UID path stays UNTESTED — it needs a real second UID (the euid check has no injection seam, by design), not producible in this environment or CI. This mirrors the Go side's cross-uid E2E gap; no vacuous green.

**Net:** item 2 fully resolved; items 1 & 3 are correctly-scoped residuals — item 1 to the GO'd XPC transport project (db24393), item 3 to a real second-UID environment.
